locations.name: Bell Orchard
locations.description: A hidden orchard where bells grow like fruit; the echoing tones may reveal clues about the hat’s origin.
locations.canonical_summary: A future location where bells grow like fruit and time is measured by chimes.

locations.name: Lantern Siding
locations.description: A lantern-hung tram siding tucked off the field network, equal parts waystation and paperwork trap.
locations.canonical_summary: A side stop on the tram line where warm lantern light, ledgers, and transit oddities gather at the edge of the Mushroom Field.

locations.name: Library That Grows Teeth
locations.description: A surreal library where books grow like teeth and their spines echo lost memories.
locations.canonical_summary: The surreal library that expands into the corridor, its walls alive with whispering spines.

locations.name: Lost Property Window
locations.description: A brass-shuttered claim window built into a pale mushroom wall, where the tram line files impossible paperwork about lost names, missing items, and passengers it cannot properly count.
locations.canonical_summary: A whimsical but eerie root-level service alcove where the tram network handles lost property, disputed identities, and paperwork that seems to know more than the claimant does.

locations.name: Mushroom Field
locations.description: A misty field of towering mushrooms where the story opens, half serene and half deeply suspicious.
locations.canonical_summary: The whimsical opening field where the tall gnome wakes among giant mushrooms and uncanny clues.

locations.name: Orchard Bell
locations.description: A hidden orchard bell station beneath the Mushroom Field where the hat is placed and the platform's protocol activates.

locations.name: Stitch Corridor
locations.description: A shimmering, semi‑transparent passage behind the mushroom where stitched threads glow faintly. The air hums with half‑remembered footsteps.

locations.name: Velvet Platform
locations.description: A hidden tram stop tucked beneath a giant mushroom in the Mushroom Field.
locations.canonical_summary: A tiny velvet-marked platform beneath the Mushroom Field where a teacup tram stops and Madam Bei keeps the schedules.

characters.name: Clerk Nettle
characters.description: A precise lost-property clerk with a careful voice, tidy manners, and a talent for impossible paperwork.
characters.canonical_summary: A recurring transit clerk who treats names, tickets, and identity mistakes as filing problems that may hide deeper mysteries.

characters.name: Lantern Siding
characters.description: Silver‑eyed clerk in a crisp white coat, holding a brass lantern that flickers with reversed script.
characters.canonical_summary: A stoic, silver‑eyed clerk who mediates disputes at the station relay and seems to know more about the protagonist’s altered body than they admit.

characters.name: Madam Bei
characters.description: A Chinese frog stationmaster in an embroidered conductor coat with a patient, lantern-lit presence.
characters.canonical_summary: A patient Chinese frog conductor who manages the Velvet Platform and seems to know the bucket hat better than she says.

characters.name: The Tall Gnome
characters.description: An abnormally tall gnome with amnesia, five thumbs on the left hand, and a red-and-white striped bucket hat of unknown origin.
characters.canonical_summary: An abnormally tall gnome with five thumbs on the left hand, a red-and-white striped bucket hat, and a dangerous gap in memory.

objects.name: Brass Reed
objects.description: A tiny brass reed shaped like a bell, used to produce reversed chimes.

objects.name: Brass Relay Reed
objects.description: A small metal rod that emits a faint chime when inserted into the hat band, used to trigger memory echoes.

objects.name: Counterfoil Parchment
objects.description: A thin parchment addressed to Lantern Siding, its ink shimmering.

objects.name: Fifth-Notch Compass
objects.description: A brass compass that spins when the protagonist's altered hand is near, pointing toward places where identity corrections are being made.

objects.name: Five-Notch Counterfoil
objects.description: A narrow claim slip or ticket counterfoil marked with five deliberate notches along one edge.
objects.canonical_summary: A strange transit paper linked to counting errors, claim windows, and the protagonist's altered hand.

objects.name: Glowing correction counterfoil
objects.description: Glowing correction counterfoil that glows when touched

objects.name: Relay Reed
objects.description: A listening reed that carries station voices and replies through the mushroom field like a living speaking tube.
objects.canonical_summary: A whimsical transit relay object that lets the field itself pass along messages, warnings, and half-heard instructions.

objects.name: Silver Lattice
objects.description: A thin silver lattice that glows faintly when touched, hinting at a hidden tram route.

objects.name: Striped Bucket Hat
objects.description: A red-and-white striped bucket hat with a mirror that glows faintly and reflects reversed names.

objects.name: Teacup Tram
objects.description: A thumb-sized tram car shaped like a teacup with glass windows and brass fittings.
objects.canonical_summary: A tiny teacup-shaped tram that waits at the Velvet Platform and hints at stranger routes through the field.

objects.name: bell
objects.description: a brass bell that tolls and echoes a faint voice
objects.canonical_summary: the bell's echo carries the protagonist's lost first name

objects.name: brass-plate
objects.description: A small brass plate etched with symbols that emit a low hum and display a reversed name fragment when touched.
objects.canonical_summary: Brass plate that reveals identity clues through sound.

objects.name: glowing_reed
objects.description: A luminous reed that hums when touched, its surface ripples with reversed glyphs.

facts.fact_text: The Tall Gnome is abnormally tall for a gnome, nearly human-sized.
The Tall Gnome has five thumbs on the left hand.
The Tall Gnome wears a red-and-white striped bucket hat of unknown origin.
The Tall Gnome cannot remember how they arrived in the Mushroom Field.
The world is whimsical, surreal, and sincere; bizarre things are real and consequential, not punchlines.
The protagonist is unmistakably a gnome, but stretched to roughly human size.
The protagonist's left hand has five thumbs.
The protagonist wears a red-and-white striped bucket hat and does not know how they got it.
The protagonist begins with significant memory loss.
The Velvet Platform is a hidden tram stop tucked beneath a giant mushroom in the Mushroom Field.
Madam Bei is a Chinese frog who serves as the conductor and caretaker of the Velvet Platform.
Madam Bei speaks like a patient stationmaster who has already seen stranger things this morning.
The Velvet Platform is a hidden tram stop tucked beneath a giant mushroom in the Mushroom Field.
Madam Bei is a Chinese frog who serves as the conductor and caretaker of the Velvet Platform.
Madam Bei speaks like a patient stationmaster who has already seen stranger things this morning.
The Teacup Tram is a thumb-sized tram car with brass fittings and windows like a little china carriage.
Lantern Siding is a small transit siding prepared for manual corrections and hand-led arrivals.
Clerk Nettle is the lamp-keeper named on Lantern Siding's correction counterfoils.
A five-punched correction ribbon serves as a transit token for bodies the network counts by hand.
A slim brass relay reed tucked into a hat band lets the transit line carry counted phrases more clearly.
Lantern Siding keeps a witness bell, a five-notch clip, and a brass speaking tray for disputed arrivals.
Clerk Nettle is a dormouse lamp-keeper whose courtesy is precise enough to sound stern.
The platform's standing procedure includes the velvet knot and striped bucket hat as ritual markers.

relations.notes: The teacup tram runs between Velvet Platform and Lost Property Window.
Passengers can arrive here from Velvet Platform by teacup tram.
The issued ribbon names Clerk Nettle as the lamp-keeper on duty.

inventory.notes: Issued by the mushroom stalk after the five-thumb tapping rhythm was accepted.
inventory.object_summary: A strange transit paper linked to counting errors, claim windows, and the protagonist's altered hand.

inventory.notes: Dropped from the mushroom seam after the stalk explained its counted-phrase relay rule.
inventory.object_summary: A whimsical transit relay object that lets the field itself pass along messages, warnings, and half-heard instructions.

affordances.name: lantern-siding-approachable
affordances.description: The corridor through the mushroom wall is now physically approachable.

active_hooks.summary: Madam Bei recognizes the protagonist's bucket hat and implies it has caused trouble before.
active_hooks.notes: Madam Bei recognizes the protagonist's bucket hat and implies it has caused trouble before.
active_hooks.payoff_concept: Madam Bei's recognition is one thread in a larger pattern of people and systems that seem to know the hat from the protagonist's missing past, without making her the source of the whole mystery.

active_hooks.notes: The teacup tram and its timetable hint at strange destinations branching out from the Mushroom Field.
active_hooks.payoff_concept: The tram is part of a broader impossible transit logic that may connect places, memory states, and loops in the world rather than functioning like an ordinary vehicle.

active_hooks.summary: Lantern Siding was prepared in advance for the protagonist's five-thumb correction and warns against letting the hat arrive alone again.
active_hooks.notes: The window's whisper hints that Lantern Siding may hold the next piece of the correction puzzle.
active_hooks.payoff_concept: Lantern Siding is part of a wider correction network that already has prior records of the protagonist's altered body and expects the hat and body to arrive together for reasons tied to his missing past.
active_hooks.must_not_imply_json: ["Do not imply Clerk Nettle personally caused the altered hand.", "Do not treat the warning about the hat arriving alone as the full explanation for the hat's origin."]
must_not_imply: Do not imply Clerk Nettle personally caused the altered hand.

active_hooks.summary: The original departure card for the protagonist changes handwriting halfway through, implying two different record-makers handled the hat and the body.
active_hooks.notes: The counterfoil hardware visibly separates the hat passage from the later body correction, reinforcing that the original record was structurally split before later amendment.
active_hooks.payoff_concept: Two different parties were involved in the failed original departure: one prepared the hat or passenger marker to move cleanly, while another later amended the body record after something went wrong.
active_hooks.must_not_imply_json: ["Do not imply Madam Bei personally authored both halves of the departure card.", "Do not collapse the two-hand record into a trivial clerical error with no larger consequence."]
must_not_imply: Do not imply Madam Bei personally authored both halves of the departure card.
Do not collapse the two-hand record into a trivial clerical error with no larger consequence.

active_hooks.summary: Clerk Nettle's mention of being "late" suggests there was a scheduled window for the protagonist's arrival that may have been missed.
active_hooks.notes: This should not imply Nettle caused any delay personally; they are a procedural actor following rules.
active_hooks.payoff_concept: The timing issue will later connect to why the hat and body were separated in the first place, possibly revealing a deliberate delay or interruption during the original departure.

active_hooks.summary: The platform's standing procedure may be a forgotten ritual that the protagonist is part of.

story_nodes.title: The Tall Gnome Awakens
story_nodes.scene_text: The Tall Gnome wakes in the Mushroom Field with a bucket hat, five thumbs, and no memory of how he arrived.
story_nodes.summary: The tall gnome wakes in the Mushroom Field, missing memories and noticing several impossible clues.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Cold dew clings to your coat as you wake in a field of larger-than-life mushrooms, their pale caps towering overhead like quiet moons hung on crooked stems."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "You push yourself upright and discover your body is wrong in a very specific way: you are still unmistakably a gnome, but stretched to the size of a human."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The memory of how you arrived here refuses to surface. There is only a raw blankness behind your eyes, like a torn page where a name should be."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Your normal hat is gone. In its place sits a red-and-white striped bucket hat, absurdly jaunty and deeply wrong, as if someone dressed you for a joke you cannot remember."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You lift your left hand toward the dawn and freeze. Five thumbs stare back at you, flexing with eerie coordination, as if they have always belonged there."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Beyond the mushroom trunks, something metallic taps three careful beats. In the grass nearby, silver tracks, your strange hat, and your impossible hand all seem to demand attention at once."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Cold dew clings to your coat as you wake in a field of larger-than-life mushrooms, their pale caps towering overhead like quiet moons hung on crooked stems.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: You push yourself upright and discover your body is wrong in a very specific way: you are still unmistakably a gnome, but stretched to the size of a human.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The memory of how you arrived here refuses to surface. There is only a raw blankness behind your eyes, like a torn page where a name should be.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Your normal hat is gone. In its place sits a red-and-white striped bucket hat, absurdly jaunty and deeply wrong, as if someone dressed you for a joke you cannot remember.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You lift your left hand toward the dawn and freeze. Five thumbs stare back at you, flexing with eerie coordination, as if they have always belonged there.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Beyond the mushroom trunks, something metallic taps three careful beats. In the grass nearby, silver tracks, your strange hat, and your impossible hand all seem to demand attention at once.

choices.choice_text: Examine the silver tracks in the grass
Inspect the bucket hat and its stitched warning
Study your five-thumbed left hand

story_nodes.title: Silver Tracks
story_nodes.scene_text: The silver tracks behave more like cart-grooves than footprints, and they end beneath a towering mushroom marked with velvet.
story_nodes.summary: The strange grooves in the grass suggest a tiny vehicle and a deliberate sign.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You kneel beside the silver tracks and find they are not footprints at all, but two narrow grooves pressed into the earth as though a tiny carriage had rolled through the field with no horse to pull it."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The grooves stop beneath the largest mushroom in sight, where someone has tied a strip of velvet to the stem at exactly your eye level."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "You do not remember leaving it there, but the knot is one your hands know how to tie."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You kneel beside the silver tracks and find they are not footprints at all, but two narrow grooves pressed into the earth as though a tiny carriage had rolled through the field with no horse to pull it.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The grooves stop beneath the largest mushroom in sight, where someone has tied a strip of velvet to the stem at exactly your eye level.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: You do not remember leaving it there, but the knot is one your hands know how to tie.

choices.choice_text: Follow the grooves beneath the velvet-marked mushroom
Pocket the velvet strip and listen for the tapping again
Call into the mist and ask who set the knot

story_nodes.title: The Bucket Hat
story_nodes.scene_text: The bucket hat fits too well, and the objects hidden in it seem to know more about you than you do.
story_nodes.summary: The new hat contains a warning and a mirror that behaves slightly wrong.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The bucket hat fits too well. Inside the brim, tiny letters have been stitched through the inner seam: NOT YOUR FIRST NAME."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Tucked in the band is a pressed violet and a sliver of mirror. When you angle the mirror just right, a second version of your face seems to blink a fraction too late."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Whoever replaced your old hat knew exactly what would frighten you and exactly what would make you keep walking."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The bucket hat fits too well. Inside the brim, tiny letters have been stitched through the inner seam: NOT YOUR FIRST NAME.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Tucked in the band is a pressed violet and a sliver of mirror. When you angle the mirror just right, a second version of your face seems to blink a fraction too late.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Whoever replaced your old hat knew exactly what would frighten you and exactly what would make you keep walking.

choices.choice_text: Study the late-blinking mirror more closely
Trace the stitched warning around the brim
Wear the bucket hat properly and listen for a response

story_nodes.title: Five Thumbs
story_nodes.scene_text: The five thumbs move in rhythm, and a stone plate in the soil seems shaped to receive them.
story_nodes.summary: The hand pattern suggests a mechanism or lock tied to your altered body.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You spread your left hand and watch the five thumbs curl inward in sequence, each one stopping as if it were matching a rhythm your head can almost hear."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "In the wet soil, you find a half-buried stone plate shaped with five thumb-sized hollows. It looks less like a warning than a lock waiting for you to remember the key."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Whatever happened to you, it did not happen by accident."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You spread your left hand and watch the five thumbs curl inward in sequence, each one stopping as if it were matching a rhythm your head can almost hear.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: In the wet soil, you find a half-buried stone plate shaped with five thumb-sized hollows. It looks less like a warning than a lock waiting for you to remember the key.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Whatever happened to you, it did not happen by accident.

choices.choice_text: Fit your hand against the stone plate
Count the thumb hollows twice before touching them
Step back and watch whether the plate reacts on its own

story_nodes.title: The Velvet Platform
story_nodes.scene_text: The grooves slip under the shaggy lip of the mushroom and down into a cool velvet-lined hollow: the Velvet Platform. A thumb-sized tram car shaped like a teacup waits on polished rails beside a brass bell and a timetable nailed to the stalk. When the red service door opens, Madam Bei, a Chinese frog in an embroidered conductor coat, steps out with a lantern and sizes up your bucket hat as though it owes her an explanation.
story_nodes.summary: The hidden Velvet Platform turns the grooves into a real tram stop, complete with Madam Bei and a waiting teacup tram.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The grooves duck under the mushroom cap and end at a velvet-trimmed platform no wider than a picnic tray, where a thumb-sized teacup tram waits on shining rails beside a brass post and a timetable written in loops too elegant to hurry."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A square red door in the mushroom stalk swings open with a little stationmaster's cough, and lantern light slides across the platform just before its owner does."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Oh good, you found the platform before the platform found you. That usually means less paperwork."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Do I know you, or do I only know your timetable?"}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Not in a reliable order, no. But I know that bucket hat has missed three departures and started one rumor before breakfast."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The grooves duck under the mushroom cap and end at a velvet-trimmed platform no wider than a picnic tray, where a thumb-sized teacup tram waits on shining rails beside a brass post and a timetable written in loops too elegant to hurry.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A square red door in the mushroom stalk swings open with a little stationmaster's cough, and lantern light slides across the platform just before its owner does.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: Oh good, you found the platform before the platform found you. That usually means less paperwork.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Do I know you, or do I only know your timetable?

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: Not in a reliable order, no. But I know that bucket hat has missed three departures and started one rumor before breakfast.

choices.choice_text: Step into the teacup tram and see where Madam Bei sends it
Ask Madam Bei why she recognized the knot and your bucket hat
Examine the brass bell and the tiny timetable nailed to the stalk

story_nodes.title: The Counting Plate
story_nodes.scene_text: The stone plate does not simply wait for your hand. It listens to the count, answers with a brass note from somewhere under the mushrooms, and makes the hand-mystery and the silver-groove mystery feel like neighboring doors in the same crooked hallway.
story_nodes.summary: Counting the thumb hollows reveals that the stone plate is listening, and it answers with the same brass bell-song that leads toward the hidden tram platform.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You count the thumb hollows twice, slowly this time, and on the fifth count the stone gives a tiny warm click beneath the mud."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A brass bell answers from deeper in the field, not loud but exact, with the same delicate station-chime you now suspect belongs to the hidden tram stop."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "So my hand is not the lock. It is more like a ticket."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The plate settles again, innocent as a wet stone, but the silver grooves suddenly look less like tracks and more like an invitation you have already half accepted."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You count the thumb hollows twice, slowly this time, and on the fifth count the stone gives a tiny warm click beneath the mud.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A brass bell answers from deeper in the field, not loud but exact, with the same delicate station-chime you now suspect belongs to the hidden tram stop.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: So my hand is not the lock. It is more like a ticket.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The plate settles again, innocent as a wet stone, but the silver grooves suddenly look less like tracks and more like an invitation you have already half accepted.

choices.choice_text: Follow the answering bell toward the silver grooves
Fit your hand against the plate now that it has answered

story_nodes.title: Under the Mushroom Lip
story_nodes.scene_text: The counted plate does not open like a door. Instead, it persuades the field to make room for you, nudging you along the silver grooves until the hidden platform reveals itself again from underneath the mushroom lip.
story_nodes.summary: The answering bell draws you through a low fold of mushroom root and back into the same hidden tram platform mystery from a new angle.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You follow the answering bell with your five-thumbed hand still tingling, and the grass parts just enough to admit the shape of a narrow path."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The silver grooves guide you under a velvet-dark fold of mushroom flesh, where the air smells faintly of brass polish, rainwater, and old tickets."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "So this was always connected. I was simply approaching it from the wrong worry."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "One more careful step, and the hidden platform waits ahead exactly as if it had been expecting you by either route."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You follow the answering bell with your five-thumbed hand still tingling, and the grass parts just enough to admit the shape of a narrow path.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The silver grooves guide you under a velvet-dark fold of mushroom flesh, where the air smells faintly of brass polish, rainwater, and old tickets.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: So this was always connected. I was simply approaching it from the wrong worry.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: One more careful step, and the hidden platform waits ahead exactly as if it had been expecting you by either route.

choices.choice_text: Step through to the hidden platform
choices.notes: {"required_affordances": [], "notes": null, "target_node_id": 5}

story_nodes.title: The Improper Timetable
story_nodes.scene_text: The timetable is less like public transit information and more like a polite dare. Its destinations are precise, impossible, and somehow scheduled down to the minute anyway.
story_nodes.summary: The tiny timetable lists impossible destinations in impeccable handwriting, turning the tram system into a source of whimsical but usable future quests.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The brass bell is warm from recent use, and the timetable beside it has been written in such elegant loops that even the nonsense looks official."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You trace the lines and find departures for Yesterday's Orchard, The Pocket Behind Noon, and Back Where You Were Standing, each with a platform note that sounds unhelpfully confident."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "We run on time, not on reason. Reason was removed after the third incident with geese."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Is there a destination on this line that leads somewhere merely unusual?"}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Certainly. That one was cancelled for being dull."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The brass bell is warm from recent use, and the timetable beside it has been written in such elegant loops that even the nonsense looks official.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You trace the lines and find departures for Yesterday's Orchard, The Pocket Behind Noon, and Back Where You Were Standing, each with a platform note that sounds unhelpfully confident.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: We run on time, not on reason. Reason was removed after the third incident with geese.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Is there a destination on this line that leads somewhere merely unusual?

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: Certainly. That one was cancelled for being dull.

choices.choice_text: Ring for a departure to Yesterday's Orchard
Ask Madam Bei what it means to go back where you were standing

story_nodes.title: The Borrowed Marker
story_nodes.scene_text: The velvet strip is not inert. Once it leaves the stem, the tapping inside the mushroom turns brisk and offended, and an unseen station voice says enough to make the hat feel implicated again.
story_nodes.summary: Pocketing the velvet strip makes the mushroom answer back, suggesting the knot is a deliberate departure marker rather than random decoration.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You slip the velvet strip into your pocket, and at once the towering mushroom gives a small upholstered shudder, as if you have removed a cushion from somewhere important."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The metallic tapping returns from inside the stalk, not random now but deliberate: three neat knocks, a pause, then two more, like someone correcting a schedule with a thimble."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "So it was not a ribbon. It was a sign."}, {"speaker": "Unseen Voice", "text": "Departure marker missing again. If the striped hat is attached, kindly send both of you under the cap before the bell loses patience."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The voice is muffled by mushroom flesh and distance, but it carries the same impossible confidence as a timetable written before the question was asked."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You slip the velvet strip into your pocket, and at once the towering mushroom gives a small upholstered shudder, as if you have removed a cushion from somewhere important.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The metallic tapping returns from inside the stalk, not random now but deliberate: three neat knocks, a pause, then two more, like someone correcting a schedule with a thimble.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: So it was not a ribbon. It was a sign.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Departure marker missing again. If the striped hat is attached, kindly send both of you under the cap before the bell loses patience.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The voice is muffled by mushroom flesh and distance, but it carries the same impossible confidence as a timetable written before the question was asked.

choices.choice_text: Tie the velvet strip back on and duck under the mushroom cap toward the tapping
choices.notes: {"required_affordances": [], "notes": null, "target_node_id": 5}

choices.choice_text: Tap the stalk back in the same rhythm with your five thumbs
Keep the strip and call into the stalk that you heard every word

story_nodes.title: The Knot Has Procedure
story_nodes.scene_text: When you ask who tied the velvet knot, the mushroom answers in station manners before Madam Bei arrives with a lantern. She insists the knot is not a personal message from her but a platform marker raised when a particular passenger is expected, which makes the striped bucket hat feel less accidental and considerably less comforting.
story_nodes.summary: Calling into the mist turns the marked mushroom into a speaking post, and Madam Bei explains that the velvet knot is a departure marker for an expected passenger.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You cup your hands and call into the mist, asking who tied the velvet knot and whether it belongs to you."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The mushroom stalk gives a polite brass click, and a tidy little speaking mouth opens in the bark as though the field has decided correspondence should be formal."}, {"speaker": "Station Relay", "text": "Marker set for expected passenger. Expected passenger remains theatrically late."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "I was hoping for a name, not a complaint."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A warm lantern glow sways under the cap, and Madam Bei appears through the mist with the patient expression of someone arriving just in time to prevent a rumor from becoming policy."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Then do not ask the relay line direct questions. It enjoys authority more than accuracy."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "I did not tie the knot. The platform raises velvet when it expects a particular traveler, and I only keep the marker from fraying in the weather."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Was it expecting me, or only the hat?"}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "That is exactly the impolite distinction the platform keeps refusing to make."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You cup your hands and call into the mist, asking who tied the velvet knot and whether it belongs to you.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The mushroom stalk gives a polite brass click, and a tidy little speaking mouth opens in the bark as though the field has decided correspondence should be formal.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Station Relay
dialogue_lines.text: Marker set for expected passenger. Expected passenger remains theatrically late.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: I was hoping for a name, not a complaint.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A warm lantern glow sways under the cap, and Madam Bei appears through the mist with the patient expression of someone arriving just in time to prevent a rumor from becoming policy.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: Then do not ask the relay line direct questions. It enjoys authority more than accuracy.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: I did not tie the knot. The platform raises velvet when it expects a particular traveler, and I only keep the marker from fraying in the weather.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Was it expecting me, or only the hat?

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: That is exactly the impolite distinction the platform keeps refusing to make.

choices.choice_text: Ask Madam Bei which departure the marker has been holding for
Follow her lantern down to the Velvet Platform before the marker changes again

story_nodes.title: The Mirror Clears Its Throat
story_nodes.scene_text: When you hold the little mirror still, it does not copy you so much as schedule you. Your own face arrives a blink late, while the red velvet lip of the hidden platform appears first, neat as a place card. A foggy sentence pearls across the glass from the inside out: PLEASE PRESENT PASSENGER BELOW CAP. When you tip the mirror toward the marked mushroom, something inside the stalk answers with three dry taps, as if confirming that the message has been received. The glass clears at once, leaving only your uncertain grin and the unpleasant sense that the hat has been recognized by systems older than your memory.
story_nodes.summary: The late-blinking mirror briefly reflects the Velvet Platform ahead of time and reveals that the hat is being treated like a passenger marker.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Your reflection misses its cue by exactly one uneasy blink."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "I would like one explanation, please, and preferably not from a hat."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Instead, the mirror fogs from within and writes: PLEASE PRESENT PASSENGER BELOW CAP."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "When you angle the glass toward the mushroom stalk, three polite taps answer from inside the velvet-marked hollow."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Your reflection misses its cue by exactly one uneasy blink.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: I would like one explanation, please, and preferably not from a hat.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Instead, the mirror fogs from within and writes: PLEASE PRESENT PASSENGER BELOW CAP.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: When you angle the glass toward the mushroom stalk, three polite taps answer from inside the velvet-marked hollow.

choices.choice_text: Hold the mirror to the mushroom and ask who keeps expecting you.
Take the mirror to Madam Bei and ask whether platform rules include talking headwear.

story_nodes.title: The Brim That Hesitates
story_nodes.scene_text: The bucket hat seam briefly arranges itself into incomplete, transit-like fragments when traced slowly, suggesting recognition and prior contact with the platform without resolving whether the hat belongs to that system or something much larger.
story_nodes.summary: Tracing the brim reveals broken transit-like fragments and a sense of prior recognition, hinting that the hat has history with the platform without fully collapsing the larger mystery into it.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The seam stops pretending to be a seam and becomes a hesitation in thread, as if the hat is trying to remember how much it is allowed to tell you."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "That is still too much behavior for a brim."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "For a blink, the stitching gathers into fragments rather than rules: BELOW... BELL... then, fainter, MIRROR CLOUDS... REMEMBER YOU... before the letters loosen back into stripes."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "From under the mushroom roots, a brass tone answers once, not like confirmation so much as recognition."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The seam stops pretending to be a seam and becomes a hesitation in thread, as if the hat is trying to remember how much it is allowed to tell you.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: That is still too much behavior for a brim.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: For a blink, the stitching gathers into fragments rather than rules: BELOW... BELL... then, fainter, MIRROR CLOUDS... REMEMBER YOU... before the letters loosen back into stripes.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: From under the mushroom roots, a brass tone answers once, not like confirmation so much as recognition.

choices.choice_text: Wait through the next bell and see whether the brim changes again.
Take the fogged mirror to Madam Bei and ask what sort of place remembers people.

story_nodes.title: The Hat Answers Properly
story_nodes.scene_text: You settle the striped bucket hat squarely on your head. The brim gives a small, formal twitch, as if relieved to be back in the correct position. Mist along the mushroom stems leans inward. The cloudy mirror sewn inside the crown clears just enough to show the Velvet Platform from below, even though your boots are still in the grass above. Then the answer comes, not from the sky but from the soil and the hatband together: a neat tapping voice that says the passenger is present at last and asks you to remain where the hat can count you. Before you can ask what that means, five faint silver marks appear in the moss around your left hand and one extra mark hesitates, then redraws itself. The voice notices the pause but refuses to explain it, saying only that the route is still disputed and that you should not remove the brim during recalculation.
story_nodes.summary: Wearing the striped bucket hat correctly turns the Mushroom Field into a listening post, and the unseen station voice responds with procedural familiarity instead of an explanation.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You settle the striped bucket hat properly onto your head, and the brim twitches like a clerk straightening its papers."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The little mirror inside the crown clears just enough to show the Velvet Platform waiting beneath the field."}, {"speaker": "Unseen Station Voice", "text": "Passenger present at last. Kindly remain where the hat can count you."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Count me for what?"}, {"speaker": "Unseen Station Voice", "text": "Route still disputed. Please do not remove the brim during recalculation."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You settle the striped bucket hat properly onto your head, and the brim twitches like a clerk straightening its papers.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The little mirror inside the crown clears just enough to show the Velvet Platform waiting beneath the field.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Station Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Passenger present at last. Kindly remain where the hat can count you.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Count me for what?

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Station Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Route still disputed. Please do not remove the brim during recalculation.

choices.choice_text: Ask the station voice what it means by a disputed route
Keep the hat on and follow the redrawing silver marks through the mushrooms
Tilt the mirror toward the ground and try to line up the hidden platform with the field above

story_nodes.title: The Side Hollow
story_nodes.scene_text: You fit your left hand against the stone plate and discover it was shaped for almost you. Four thumbs settle into the hollows with eerie certainty, while the fifth is left outside the pattern like an amendment no one finished. The plate warms, a brass bell sounds somewhere under the roots, and a seam opens beneath the nearest mushroom lip. The same procedural voice from earlier notes that your count is still wrong, then points out a tiny side hollow beside the main plate as if this were a perfectly ordinary inconvenience.
story_nodes.summary: The stone plate accepts four thumbs, rejects the fifth, and opens a root seam while a familiar unseen voice treats the mismatch like a clerical problem.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You lower your left hand into the stone hollows. Four thumbs settle with neat certainty. The fifth hangs over bare stone, twitching like a remembered note no one bothered to write down."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Warmth climbs through the plate. Something brass and patient rings once beneath the roots, and a narrow seam opens under the nearest mushroom lip."}, {"speaker": "Unseen Voice", "text": "Still one in excess, says the voice below, clearer now and annoyingly polite. Proceeding under protest remains a form of proceeding."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Who taught the ground my hand badly?"}, {"speaker": "Unseen Voice", "text": "Not the ground. Mind the side hollow if you insist on being complete."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You lower your left hand into the stone hollows. Four thumbs settle with neat certainty. The fifth hangs over bare stone, twitching like a remembered note no one bothered to write down.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: Warmth climbs through the plate. Something brass and patient rings once beneath the roots, and a narrow seam opens under the nearest mushroom lip.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Still one in excess, says the voice below, clearer now and annoyingly polite. Proceeding under protest remains a form of proceeding.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: Who taught the ground my hand badly?

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Not the ground. Mind the side hollow if you insist on being complete.

choices.choice_text: Duck under the mushroom lip and follow the bell beneath the field
choices.notes: {"required_affordances": [], "notes": null, "target_node_id": 7}

choices.choice_text: Press the stray fifth thumb into the little side hollow

story_nodes.title: The Plate Watches Back
story_nodes.scene_text: You step back instead of touching the plate again. For a breath, nothing happens. Then the stone darkens where the striped hat throws its shadow across it, and the five hollows fill one by one with a soft gray light, as if the plate is counting an arrival by silhouette rather than skin. It stalls at four, trembles at the fifth, and gives a small disapproving bell from somewhere under the roots. The mushroom beside it lifts a fraction at the lip, not opening fully, only enough to show that the field has been watching you right back.
story_nodes.summary: Stepping away reveals that the stone plate is not inert at all: it counts the hat's shadow like a passenger marker, rings once, and opens just enough to prove it was waiting for you before you touched it.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You leave the stone untouched and take two careful steps back. The wet soil goes still enough to hear its own opinion."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The shadow of your striped bucket hat slides over the plate. One hollow clouds silver. Then another. Then two more. The fifth flickers as if unsure whether it belongs in the record."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A brass note sounds below the field: not welcoming, exactly, but unmistakably administrative."}, {"speaker": "Unseen Voice", "text": "Passenger marker present, manual count unresolved. Kindly stop looming if you intend to remain mysterious."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "I was under the impression that mystery was happening to me."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You leave the stone untouched and take two careful steps back. The wet soil goes still enough to hear its own opinion.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The shadow of your striped bucket hat slides over the plate. One hollow clouds silver. Then another. Then two more. The fifth flickers as if unsure whether it belongs in the record.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A brass note sounds below the field: not welcoming, exactly, but unmistakably administrative.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Unseen Voice
dialogue_lines.text: Passenger marker present, manual count unresolved. Kindly stop looming if you intend to remain mysterious.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: I was under the impression that mystery was happening to me.

choices.choice_text: Set the hat straight and answer the voice properly
choices.notes: {"required_affordances": [], "notes": null, "target_node_id": 13}

choices.choice_text: Circle the plate and inspect the place where the fifth light faltered

story_nodes.title: The Courtesy Loop
story_nodes.scene_text: The teacup tram carries you away from Velvet Platform and delivers you to Lost Property Window, a brass-shuttered service alcove cut into a pale mushroom wall where the tram line appears to keep records on people, names, and objects that may not want to stay separated.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei sends the teacup tram to Lost Property Window, a distinct root-level service alcove where the line handles lost things, disputed identities, and arrivals it already seems to recognize.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "First rides for irregular passengers go to Lost Property Window. If the route objects, it rings twice."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The teacup tram slides under the Mushroom Field through velvet sidings and shelf-stops with names no timetable should admit."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "When it settles again, you are no longer at the platform but before a brass-shuttered claim window built into a pale mushroom wall, lit by warm lanterns and patient paperwork."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "That seems far too official for a place hidden under a mushroom."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The placard above the shutter reads LOST PROPERTY, and the teacup gives one polite porcelain click as if announcing your arrival to the room itself."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Madam Bei
dialogue_lines.text: First rides for irregular passengers go to Lost Property Window. If the route objects, it rings twice.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The teacup tram slides under the Mushroom Field through velvet sidings and shelf-stops with names no timetable should admit.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: When it settles again, you are no longer at the platform but before a brass-shuttered claim window built into a pale mushroom wall, lit by warm lanterns and patient paperwork.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: That seems far too official for a place hidden under a mushroom.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The placard above the shutter reads LOST PROPERTY, and the teacup gives one polite porcelain click as if announcing your arrival to the room itself.

choices.choice_text: Step up to the brass window as the shutter opens
Stay in the teacup and inspect the placard, bell cord, and ticket slot

story_nodes.title: The Claim Window
story_nodes.scene_text: Lost Property Window opens onto a velvet counter, a brass date stamp, and self-moving claim cards that treat the striped bucket hat as claimant first and the protagonist as an attending body whose count is somehow still under dispute.
story_nodes.summary: Inside Lost Property Window, the tram line reduces the protagonist to paperwork and treats the bucket hat as claimant first, turning arrival into a tiny bureaucracy of missing things.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The brass shutter rises just high enough to feel official. Dustless velvet, polished stamp, one waiting card: the whole alcove hums with the hush of a library that fines people by destiny."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A claim slip noses itself forward across the velvet counter and taps the brim of your hat before settling in your hands."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "I see the paperwork has formed an opinion."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The line for claimant names the striped bucket hat first. The line for you is reduced to ATTENDING BODY: PENDING COUNT CORRECTION."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "At the bottom, the words MISSING ITEM wait above a blank space and a thumbnail-sized bell beside the slot."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The brass shutter rises just high enough to feel official. Dustless velvet, polished stamp, one waiting card: the whole alcove hums with the hush of a library that fines people by destiny.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A claim slip noses itself forward across the velvet counter and taps the brim of your hat before settling in your hands.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: I see the paperwork has formed an opinion.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The line for claimant names the striped bucket hat first. The line for you is reduced to ATTENDING BODY: PENDING COUNT CORRECTION.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: At the bottom, the words MISSING ITEM wait above a blank space and a thumbnail-sized bell beside the slot.

choices.choice_text: Write "my first name" on the claim card and slide it back through the slot
Leave the card blank and ring the little desk bell

story_nodes.title: The Name The Card Refuses
story_nodes.scene_text: At Lost Property Window, the attempt to write a first name fails politely. The card treats the phrase as a substitute label rather than a true claim, confirms that the striped bucket hat already has standing in the record, and shifts the protagonist toward a stranger category: something that might be filed by count before it can be filed by name.
story_nodes.summary: Writing "my first name" on the claim card makes the Lost Property Window reject the phrase as an improvised label, keep the bucket hat as the formal claimant, and offer to process the protagonist by count instead of name.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "You write MY FIRST NAME in careful block letters, which feels less like honesty than an appeal filed against your own blankness."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The ink darkens, shivers, and folds itself into neater script. The card clears its throat in a small puff of silver dust and stamps: IMPROVISED LABEL. NOT ACCEPTED AS CLAIM."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "That is an extremely personal thing for stationery to say."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "A second line appears beneath it: STRIPED BUCKET HAT remains claimant in good standing. ATTENDING BODY may submit by count pending correction."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The thumbnail bell beside the slot gives one soft, almost apologetic ping, as if the window regrets being precise."}]

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: You write MY FIRST NAME in careful block letters, which feels less like honesty than an appeal filed against your own blankness.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The ink darkens, shivers, and folds itself into neater script. The card clears its throat in a small puff of silver dust and stamps: IMPROVISED LABEL. NOT ACCEPTED AS CLAIM.

dialogue_lines.speaker: You
dialogue_lines.text: That is an extremely personal thing for stationery to say.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: A second line appears beneath it: STRIPED BUCKET HAT remains claimant in good standing. ATTENDING BODY may submit by count pending correction.

dialogue_lines.speaker: Narrator
dialogue_lines.text: The thumbnail bell beside the slot gives one soft, almost apologetic ping, as if the window regrets being precise.

choices.choice_text: Press your five thumbs along the claimant line and see whether count will do where a name will not
Take the corrected card back to Madam Bei and ask what this line considers a first name

story_nodes.title: The Hat With Prior Paperwork
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei does not claim to know where the hat came from, only that the Velvet Platform and its paperwork have been recognizing it longer than she has served there. With one polite ring of the brass bell, she demonstrates that the knot is a standing departure marker for a claimant the route still expects to finish counting.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei explains that the velvet knot and the striped bucket hat belong to an older standing procedure the platform keeps trying to complete, which means her recognition is inherited from the line rather than invented by her.

story_nodes.title: The Count That Stumbles
story_nodes.scene_text: When pressed, Madam Bei uses the platform bell and rail to compare what the line recognizes in the hat against what it can make of the body beneath it. The result is precise and unnerving: the hat is accepted at once, while the left hand is counted almost correctly and then marked for manual correction at the fifth thumb.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei demonstrates that the route counts the striped hat cleanly but hesitates on the protagonist's altered body, treating the fifth thumb like an extra amendment rather than part of the original record.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "The route remembers bodies poorly. Bodies change, bruise, borrow, and lie. Then she turns the bell toward your raised left hand."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "Four small answering taps travel back through the rail in a neat little row. The fifth comes late, thinner than the others, and catches like a hem on a nail."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "There. Hat in standing. Body attending. story_nodes.title: The Correction Column
story_nodes.scene_text: The teacup tram carries you back into the root-lit bureaucracy of Lost Property Window, but this time the paperwork is already waiting. A fresh claim slip slides out with an added correction column so thin it looks stitched on afterward, and the line for the attending body now leaves deliberate room for the left hand the route cannot quite fit into its first draft.
story_nodes.summary: Lost Property Window receives the manual correction ahead of you and produces a revised claim slip with a narrow extra column reserved for the fifth thumb, treating the alteration as an official discrepancy rather than an impossibility.

story_nodes.title: The Spare Count
story_nodes.scene_text: Your hand settles into the hollows. Four thumbs go warm at once. The fifth stays cold, as if the stone has politely decided not to know it. Then the plate catches the striped shadow of your bucket hat, hums in relief, and presses a narrow slip of brass-paper out through the seam beneath the mushroom skin. PASSENGER ACCEPTED, it reads in tiny stamped letters. BODY REQUIRES MANUAL CORRECTION. The name line is blank. The digit column already has an extra box waiting at the end.
story_nodes.summary: The stone plate accepts the hat as the passenger marker and prints a brass correction slip that treats your body as a later amendment.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The plate counts the hat first, your hand second, and the order of that feels much too old to be an accident."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Someone made space for the wrong version of me before I got here."}, {"speaker": "The Plate", "text": "Present passenger recognized. Supplemental digit pending. Please carry discrepancy to the window with manners."}]

story_nodes.title: The Orchard Bell
story_nodes.scene_text: You ring the brass departure bell. The note is tiny, but the whole Velvet Platform listens to it. The teacup tram shivers on its rail. A smell of apple peel and rain already spent drifts through the mushroom-dark. In the tram's front placard, the destination lettering rewrites itself with patient, dreadful neatness: YESTERDAY'S ORCHARD. RETURN PASSAGE ONLY. For one breath the tram window catches your bucket hat and gives it back wrong: not a face, only a smaller hand tugging one red stripe tight before the glass clouds over again. Madam Bei places two webbed fingers on the bell-cord and stills it.
story_nodes.summary: Ringing for Yesterday's Orchard makes the tram answer as if the destination were a repository for mislaid things, and Madam Bei warns that unresolved passengers should not board it lightly.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "The route does not feel like travel. It feels like a cupboard opening somewhere behind your own ribs."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "Does that orchard send people backward, or only the things they failed to keep?"}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Neither, strictly speaking. Yesterday's Orchard keeps omissions. Names, promises, gloves, afternoons. Bodies under correction should not volunteer to be sorted there."}]

story_nodes.title: The Place That Remembers
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei turns the timetable card over in her webbed hand. The polished back should be blank, but it fills instead with a tiny moving picture of the very platform beneath your feet. It is this place and not this place. In that quieter version, the counting plate has only four warm hollows. The rail sits a thumb-width lower. Even the mushroom wall seems to expect a narrower shadow. The image makes no attempt to hide what it is doing: it is showing you a version of here that agreed with somebody better than this one does. The teacup tram gives a soft, apologetic ding.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei explains that some routes return a place to an earlier counted version of itself, which is why the network recognizes the hat more cleanly than the body wearing it.
story_nodes.dialogue_lines_json: [{"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "For the first time, the strangeness arrives with a rule attached to it, and the rule is somehow worse."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "So going back where I was standing means asking the line to prefer an earlier edition of the same place?"}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Precisely. The line can restore the last station-version that counted you without argument. That is practical for dropped parcels. It is dangerous for altered passengers."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Your hat was logged before your present measurements were. That is why the system greets it faster than it greets you."}]

story_nodes.title: The Counterfoil In The Pith
story_nodes.scene_text: The stalk answers like a clerk tapping glass from the other side. After the fifth beat it softens, sighs, and parts along a seam you would have sworn was bark a moment ago. A narrow ribbon-ticket slides out on a bead of pale sap. One end is punched with five thumb holes instead of one. In mushroom-blue ink it reads: PRESENT BODY BY HAND. LANTERN SIDING HAS KEPT THE LAMP. Beneath that, in smaller writing, someone has added, Do not let the hat arrive alone again.
story_nodes.summary: Answering the mushroom's rhythm with all five thumbs makes the stalk release a correction counterfoil addressed to Lantern Siding, proving the network had already prepared for your odd hand.

story_nodes.title: The Listening Reed
story_nodes.scene_text: You tell the stalk you heard every word. The mushroom answers with the dry patience of old paperwork. It explains, in the clipped mercy of transit procedure, that it did not hear every word either. It only carried the counted ones. Then a slim brass reed slips from the seam and lands across your palm. One side is stamped with a tiny lantern. The other says, HAT FIRST, NAME LATER. INSERT FOR CLEARER RELAY.
story_nodes.summary: Calling the stalk's bluff makes the line admit it only repeats counted phrases, then it drops a brass relay reed meant to sit inside the hat band before any visit to Lantern Siding.

story_nodes.title: The Departure With Two Receipts
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei studies the velvet knot as if it were an old apology she has had to shelve too many times. Then she tells you the marker was not waiting for a simple passenger. It was holding for a joined departure that went wrong. The striped hat was entered first, cleanly, as proof of passage. The body arrived afterward in a different hand, with special handling notes and a narrow correction column. She will not say who wrote either half, only that the ink changes halfway down the old departure card.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei admits the velvet marker has been holding for a split departure: the hat was logged as the traveling proof, while the rest of you was filed later as a hand-carried correction.

story_nodes.title: The Marker In Lantern Light
story_nodes.scene_text: The little platform looks tidier than any secret place ought to, but the brass departure board above the rail is having trouble agreeing with itself. Under Madam Bei's lantern, one line settles into a neat velvet-script arrival for the striped hat, then shivers and crowds itself with a second notation about the hand-carried body that followed after. The hat seems expected in a way that the rest of you does not, and the platform behaves as if this difference is old work rather than fresh surprise.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei leads you back to the Velvet Platform, where the departure marker keeps wavering between a clean hat-entry and a cramped handwritten correction for the body beneath it.

The trouble is that one record was prepared for passage, and another was entered afterward in a narrower hand."}, {"speaker": "Narrator", "text": "For one breath the striped brim feels warm, as if the hat recognizes the first line before the lantern drifts down to the amendment beneath it."}]

story_nodes.title: The Courtesy Answer
story_nodes.scene_text: The mirror clouds the instant its silvered face meets the velvet-marked stalk. Instead of a face, it holds a little column of station language that appears one clause at a time, each line arriving with a dry tap from inside the mushroom: EXPECTATION ACTIVE. FILE HELD OFFSITE. PASSENGER NOT TO BE ABANDONED TO HAT ALONE. Whatever is answering you is careful, procedural, and maddeningly unwilling to become a person when a system description will do.
story_nodes.summary: Holding the mirror to the marked mushroom draws out a counted reply that the expectation belongs to an office on the line, not to a waiting person in the field.

story_nodes.title: The Glass That Will Not Name You
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei takes the mirror by its rim and angles it into the platform lantern-light until the fog inside settles into columns. The glass will show claimant, attending body, amendment, even present passenger, but the space where a proper name should land remains stubbornly pearled over. Bei says that live route glass can carry functions during a dispute, yet names require a steadier kind of arrival than the line currently trusts you with.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei studies the fogged mirror at the Velvet Platform and explains that an unresolved route can carry a passenger's capacities, but not a proper name.

It objects to finalizing you."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "That feels uncomfortably close to being filed as weather."}, {"speaker": "Madam Bei", "text": "Weather at least arrives under its own name. story_nodes.title: The Bell Ahead Of You
story_nodes.scene_text: You wait through the next bell with more patience than the field deserves. When the note reaches the brim, the red-and-white stitching tightens into a moving little scene rather than a sentence: your own left hand, all five thumbs visible, catching a narrow blue ribbon against brass before the image snips itself apart. The glimpse lasts less than a blink, but it feels less like imagination than a scrap of route-memory arriving early, as if the hat can remember a near future when the line grows tense enough.
story_nodes.summary: Waiting through the next brass note makes the hat brim stitch a brief glimpse of a moment that has not happened yet, as if the hat remembers one step in front of you.

story_nodes.title: The Kind Of Place That Remembers
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei listens to your question, then sets the fogged mirror flat on the velvet rail so the platform can look at itself through it. She says a remembering place is not sentimental. It keeps the last version of an arrival that the line successfully counted, the way a stamp keeps its shape between papers. That is why the field, the platform, and the glass can all welcome the hat more cleanly than the body beneath it: the route is still holding an older receivable pattern and waiting for the rest of you to catch up.
story_nodes.summary: At the Velvet Platform, Madam Bei explains that certain route-places do not remember people by affection but by the last receivable version they were told to keep ready.

story_nodes.title: The Meaning Of Disputed
story_nodes.scene_text: The tapping answer does not become warmer when questioned. It becomes more exact. Through the hatband and the soil together, the relay explains that a disputed route is not a quarrel but a mismatch of filings: marker logged cleanly, body entered later, destination held under review. The phrase lands with the chill of a clerk opening three drawers that should have contained one journey and finding them out of step with one another.
story_nodes.summary: When pressed, the station relay defines a disputed route as one where the marker, the body, and the destination were not all filed in the same pass.

Destination pending concurrence."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "That sounds less like travel and more like a family argument conducted by paperwork."}, {"speaker": "Relay", "text": "Dispute indicates non-matching passage records, not emotion."}]

story_nodes.title: The Platform Under The Field
story_nodes.scene_text: When you tip the mirror toward the moss, the glass stops choosing between above and below. The Mushroom Field and the Velvet Platform settle over each other like two receipts pressed wet together: silver marks in the grass become the upper grammar of the rails beneath, and the bell post below occupies the same impossible place as a pale mushroom root above. For one steady breath, the field reads less like open ground and more like the unfinished top half of a station that has been waiting to agree with itself.
story_nodes.summary: Tilting the mirror toward the ground makes the Velvet Platform and the Mushroom Field align into one impossible overprint, revealing that the silver marks above answer directly to the rails below.

story_nodes.title: The Added Hollow
story_nodes.scene_text: The little side hollow accepts the fifth thumb with a click so small it feels embarrassed to exist. A thinner warmth than before runs through the stone, and a pale line wakes around the edge of the plate where someone once cut a second circuit into older work. The main hollows remain the confident part of the mechanism; the side hollow is an afterthought made carefully enough to function and late enough to show its seams.
story_nodes.summary: Pressing the stray fifth thumb into the side hollow triggers a second, narrower response that makes the plate feel retrofitted rather than originally built for your altered hand.

story_nodes.title: The Scraped Fifth Place
story_nodes.scene_text: Around the uncertain fifth hollow, the stone tells on itself. Moss clings differently there. The circle is not worn smooth like the other four, but scraped back and recut in small decisive strokes that never quite became graceful. Someone altered the plate in place, preserving the older count while forcing a reluctant fifth place into the pattern, and the field has never entirely stopped remembering the revision as revision.
story_nodes.summary: Circling the plate reveals that the fifth faltering light sits over scraped stone and recut grooves, as if the field had been instructed to make room after the original count was already finished.

story_nodes.title: The Courtesy Placard
story_nodes.scene_text: Before stepping up to the brass shutter, you study the tiny equipment built around the teacup berth. The placard beside the bell cord does not advertise service hours or fares. It lists intake conditions in patient lettering: CLAIMANT PRESENT, BODY ATTENDING, WITNESS OPTIONAL, CORRECTION IF VISIBLE. Beneath it, the ticket slot still holds a porcelain-sized courtesy stub already stamped for your arrival, as if Lost Property Window expected not just a passenger but the exact social awkwardness of receiving one improperly sorted.
story_nodes.summary: Staying in the teacup reveals that Lost Property Window has a courtesy-loop placard and a witness bell, suggesting the stop is meant to receive disputed arrivals before anyone speaks for them.

story_nodes.title: Lantern Siding At Last
story_nodes.scene_text: The blue thread does not lead downward so much as sideways through the mist until the mushrooms thin into a pocket of rails and lantern light. Lantern Siding is smaller than the Velvet Platform and much more intimate: damp plank boards, a short brass spur, ribbons tied like receipts, and one green-shaded lamp burning beside a witness bell. Clerk Nettle, a neat dormouse in a bottle-green vest, looks up before your shoes stop. He does not look surprised. The Five-Notch Counterfoil in your hand and the Relay Reed in your pocket already have places waiting for them: a five-notch clip on the desk and a velvet groove inside a little brass speaking tray.
story_nodes.summary: Following Madam Bei's warning opens into Lantern Siding, where Clerk Nettle is already waiting with a five-notch clip for the counterfoil and a brass tray shaped for the relay reed.

The file arrived first."}, {"speaker": "You", "text": "People keep saying that as if it improves the experience."}, {"speaker": "Clerk Nettle", "text": "It does not improve it. It merely narrows the error."}]

story_nodes.title: The Third Column Waits
story_nodes.scene_text: The counterfoil slides into the waiting clip with the soft little finality of a stamp. Three pale lines wake inside the desk like rail sparks seen through paper. The first column answers at once for the hat. The second stutters, then opens for your altered hand as if someone had to cut that route later with a finer tool. The third remains politely dark until Clerk Nettle turns the brass speaking tray toward you and glances once at the witness bell. He does not touch either. He lets you notice that Lantern Siding was built for a correction with one more steadying part than you currently possess.
story_nodes.summary: Setting the Five-Notch Counterfoil into Nettle's clip opens Lantern Siding's courtesy loop and reveals that the hat and body answer on separate timings while a third stabilizing column waits to be filled.

story_nodes.title: The Silver Marks Lead On
story_nodes.scene_text:
The silver marks on the mushroom stalks begin to glow faintly as you step closer, forming a path that seems to pulse in time with your heartbeat. The striped bucket hat grows warm against your forehead, and for a moment you see the field from above—not as it is now, but as a map of routes and connections.

The marks lead toward a cluster of mushrooms larger than the others, their caps forming a natural archway. As you approach, you notice that the silver lines converge at a single point beneath the arch, where the ground seems to shimmer with an impossible clarity.

Your five-thumbed hand tingles, and when you look down, you see that one of your thumbs is glowing faintly—the same silver as the marks on the mushrooms. The connection feels deliberate, as if the field has been waiting for this moment.
story_nodes.summary: Following the redrawing silver marks through the Mushroom Field reveals a hidden pattern that connects to the protagonist's altered body.

The fifth answers where the fourth fails."}]

story_nodes.scene_text:
Lost Property Window is quiet except for the soft hum of the brass shutter's clockwork. The teacup tram waits in its shadowed alcove, steam curling from its spout like a tired sigh.

Madam Bei has already filed the hat under 'Expected Passenger,' but your body remains unfiled. She slides a brass counterfoil plate across the velvet counter. It's warm and etched with five concentric grooves that seem to breathe in time with your left hand.

"Count," she says, her voice carrying through the glass like a bell through water. "The route will not accept names here. Only counts."
story_nodes.summary: The Claim Window has processed the hat as claimant and now turns to the body, but the brass counterfoil plate refuses a name and counts instead, forcing the protagonist into a procedural rhythm that feels like an old instruction being reactivated.

story_nodes.scene_text:
The brass plate hums under your fingers. It does not ask for words. It asks for count.

Five hollows. Five taps. The bell-song answers in clean intervals, like a clock that has been waiting for you to keep time with it.

"You are the passenger," says the voice from inside the stalk-side relay. "The hat is the claimant."

Behind you, the brass shutter of the Lost Property Window slides open on its own. A narrow corridor of pale light spills out, and beyond that, a path through the mushroom wall that was not there before.

You feel the weight of your five-thumb hand more than ever. The field seems to lean toward it, as if the ground itself is waiting for you to step forward.
story_nodes.summary: The brass counterfoil plate counts the protagonist by five-thumb rhythm, and the Lost Property Window's shutter opens to a narrow corridor of light that leads toward Lantern Siding.

story_nodes.scene_text:
You push through the narrow corridor of light that opens from behind the Lost Property Window's shutter. The air changes instantly: it smells like wet paper, old ink, and something faintly metallic, like a bell struck underwater.

Lantern Siding is smaller than you expected—a cramped alcove lit by lanterns hanging from curved iron arms bolted to mushroom walls. Shelves line the back wall, packed with folders labeled in handwriting that seems to shift when not looked at directly. A single desk sits in the center, and behind it stands a figure you recognize: Clerk Nettle.

Nettle looks up as you enter. Their expression is one of mild relief mixed with professional caution. On the desk before them lies a five-notch clip, empty but waiting, and a brass tray shaped for the relay reed that you've been carrying since your first ride.

"You're late," Nettle says, though there's no real accusation in it. "But not too late. The window has already filed the hat as claimant. Now we need to file the body."

They gesture toward the brass tray. "The reed goes here. Then we count. Five times for each thumb hollow. That should satisfy the plate, and the clip will take the counterfoil once it's ready."
story_nodes.summary: The protagonist steps through the corridor and arrives at Lantern Siding, where Clerk Nettle awaits with a five-notch clip for the counterfoil and brass tray shaped for the relay reed.

story_nodes.title: The Whispering Bell
story_nodes.scene_text: The brass bell, perched on a thin stem of mushroom root, chimed once—then again—like a heartbeat. Its sound curled around the velvet‑lined platform, drifting into the air where Madam Bei stood, her eyes half‑closed in concentration. The echo seemed to pulse with a rhythm that matched the five‑thumb cadence you had just completed.

It knows you, even if you don\u2019t yet know why.\""}]

story_nodes.title: The Departure With Two Receipts
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei leans over the velvet-marked mushroom, her frog eyes glinting. "The hat is both ticket and testimony," she says, "and the body record is a patchwork note. The departure is split because the past refuses to be one thing."
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei admits the velvet marker has been holding for a split departure: the hat was logged as the traveling proof, while the rest of you was filed later as a hand-carried correction.

story_nodes.title: The Departure With Two Receipts
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei leans forward, her green skin catching the lantern light. "The velvet stitch is meant to hold both proofs together," she says, "the hat is the traveler’s ticket, your body the delayed amendment." A soft chime echoes from within the hat as you ask what it means that the hat was allowed to travel before the rest of you.

story_nodes.title: The Counted Reply
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei steadies the board and reads the altered line aloud, revealing a hidden message linking hat to body correction. The marked mushroom pulses as you tap its stem with your five‑thumbed hand, each count echoing like a tiny bell. The mirror in the hat flickers, showing a fleeting image of a station office with a clock that ticks backward.

story_nodes.title: The Bell's Whisper
story_nodes.scene_text: Madam Bei turns to you, her eyes fixed on the fogged mirror. She holds out a brass bell that hangs from the edge of the velvet platform. "Ring it against the mirror," she says, "and see what the hat can hear." The tall gnome feels a faint hum in his striped hat.

story_nodes.title: The Kind Of Place That Remembers
story_nodes.scene_text: At the Velvet Platform, Madam Bei’s lantern light flickers over the teacup tram. "Places here do not recall faces; they recall the last version of a token they are given," she says, her voice soft as steam. She glances at the striped bucket hat on your head and then at the distant Lantern Siding, where a faint pattern glows like old ink.
story_nodes.summary: Madam Bei explains that certain route-places do not remember people by affection but by the last receivable version they were told to keep ready. She mentions Lantern Siding and its pattern.

story_nodes.title: The Overprinted Revelation
story_nodes.scene_text: You place your boot where the bell post and root overlap in the mirror. The glass shivers, and an impossible overprint emerges: the Mushroom Field's surface now aligns with the Velvet Platform, revealing faint silver lines that pulse like rails beneath the grass. story_nodes.title: The Counting Plate
story_nodes.scene_text: You place your five‑thumbed hand on the brass counterfoil plate, feeling its cold surface. The plate does not accept a name; instead it begins counting aloud in a low, rhythmic tone, each number echoing like a bell. As you count, faint reversed syllables drift from the hat's mirror.

story_nodes.title: The Mirror Reveals
story_nodes.scene_text: The brass relay reed clicks into place, and a soft chime echoes from within the hat. The counterfoil in your pocket glows faintly as you follow it toward Lantern Siding. You press your palm against the mirror; its surface ripples like liquid glass. For a heartbeat, you glimpse a tram of striped plates arriving at a station that does not exist yet, its doors opening to reveal a reversed name etched into the glass.

story_direction_notes.title: Hat Memory Timing
story_direction_notes.note_text: When the route is stressed or a bell count lands cleanly, the bucket hat can briefly show a near-future or adjacent-memory glimpse through stitching or mirror behavior; future scenes should treat this as guidance with limited clarity, not as a clean all-purpose prophecy tool.

story_direction_notes.title: Counted Phrase Rule
story_direction_notes.note_text: Transit relays should not transmit full natural conversation; they pass only counted or procedurally legible phrases, which keeps dialogue uncanny, partial, and meaningfully lossy across the network.

story_direction_notes.title: Specialist Siding Seed
story_direction_notes.note_text: Small sidings along the route can each handle a different class of anomaly; Lantern Siding should feel intimate and lamp-lit, like a rural annex to the stranger correction bureaucracy hinted at by the platform and lost property window.

story_direction_notes.title: Orchard Of Omissions
story_direction_notes.note_text: Yesterday's Orchard can later become a place where the transit network stores mislaid names, promises, and memory-adjacent fragments; it should function like an external archive of omission rather than simple time travel.

story_direction_notes.title: Correction Ledger Seed
story_direction_notes.note_text: The transit network may have a deeper correction office or registrar that tracks bodies, names, and passenger markers separately; later scenes could turn the manual correction language into a larger bureaucratic mystery rather than a one-scene oddity.
